(Japan) (P3225) == * 7130005009083 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市大住池平31', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:月讀神社 (京田辺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 月讀神社 |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京田辺市|Q816728}}大住池平31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ツクヨミ|Q595520|月読命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（大） |創建 = {{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月15日|Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=月讀神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''月讀神社'''（つきよみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京田辺市|Q816728}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}。'''{{jalink|隼人舞|Q55529681}}'''の発祥地として知られる。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}）社殿を平城京より平安京に遷した際、大住山において霊光を拝し、この地に社殿を建立したのを創建とする。{{jalink|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|Q1193959}}の際は、戦火を避けるため{{jalink|石清水八幡宮|Q710098}}が一時遷座され、御神宝が薬師堂に安置された。 === 神階 === *{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から正五位下（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「樺井月読神」 *{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}6年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）、従一位（社伝） === 隼人舞 === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|隼人舞発祥之碑}}]] {{jalink|九州|Q13987}}南部の大隅{{jalink|隼人|Q1151764}}が{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}大住に移住し、この大住隼人が郷土の民俗芸能の隼人舞をこの月讀神社に奉納して舞伝えてきたという伝承から、隼人舞の発祥地とされている。{{jalink|10月14日|Q2922}}の例祭宵宮では、現在も隼人舞が奉納される（京田辺市指定{{jalink|無形民俗文化財}}である{{Cite web|和書|title=「月読神社と隼人舞」|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=京田辺市|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=京田辺市}}）。 また、当神社が所在する「大住」という地名も九州の「{{jalink|大隅国|Q981002|大隅}}」に由来と考えられる{{Cite web|和書|title=大住隼人舞|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=京田辺市教育委員会}}。 === 境内 === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|境内 === 交通アクセス === *{{jalink|学研都市線}}（{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}）{{jalink|大住駅|Q589844}}から徒歩15分 ===脚注=== {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ 大住隼人舞]-京田辺市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] (Japan) (P3225) == * 7130005009083 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市大住池平31', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:月讀神社 (京田辺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 月讀神社 |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京田辺市|Q816728}}大住池平31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ツクヨミ|Q595520|月読命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（大） |創建 = {{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月15日|Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=月讀神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''月讀神社'''（つきよみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京田辺市|Q816728}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}。'''{{jalink|隼人舞|Q55529681}}'''の発祥地として知られる。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}）社殿を平城京より平安京に遷した際、大住山において霊光を拝し、この地に社殿を建立したのを創建とする。{{jalink|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|Q1193959}}の際は、戦火を避けるため{{jalink|石清水八幡宮|Q710098}}が一時遷座され、御神宝が薬師堂に安置された。 === 神階 === *{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から正五位下（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「樺井月読神」 *{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}6年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）、従一位（社伝） === 隼人舞 === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|隼人舞発祥之碑}}]] {{jalink|九州|Q13987}}南部の大隅{{jalink|隼人|Q1151764}}が{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}大住に移住し、この大住隼人が郷土の民俗芸能の隼人舞をこの月讀神社に奉納して舞伝えてきたという伝承から、隼人舞の発祥地とされている。{{jalink|10月14日|Q2922}}の例祭宵宮では、現在も隼人舞が奉納される（京田辺市指定{{jalink|無形民俗文化財}}である{{Cite web|和書|title=「月読神社と隼人舞」|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=京田辺市|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=京田辺市}}）。 また、当神社が所在する「大住」という地名も九州の「{{jalink|大隅国|Q981002|大隅}}」に由来と考えられる{{Cite web|和書|title=大住隼人舞|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=京田辺市教育委員会}}。 === 境内 === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|境内 === 交通アクセス === *{{jalink|学研都市線}}（{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}）{{jalink|大住駅|Q589844}}から徒歩15分 ===脚注=== {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ 大住隼人舞]-京田辺市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

(日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月15日|Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=月讀神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''月讀神社'''（つきよみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京田辺市|Q816728}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}。'''{{jalink|隼人舞|Q55529681}}'''の発祥地として知られる。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}）社殿を平城京より平安京に遷した際、大住山において霊光を拝し、この地に社殿を建立したのを創建とする。{{jalink|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|Q1193959}}の際は、戦火を避けるため{{jalink|石清水八幡宮|Q710098}}が一時遷座され、御神宝が薬師堂に安置された。 === 神階 === *{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から正五位下（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「樺井月読神」 *{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}6年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）、従一位（社伝） === 隼人舞 === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|隼人舞発祥之碑}}]] {{jalink|九州|Q13987}}南部の大隅{{jalink|隼人|Q1151764}}が{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}大住に移住し、この大住隼人が郷土の民俗芸能の隼人舞をこの月讀神社に奉納して舞伝えてきたという伝承から、隼人舞の発祥地とされている。{{jalink|10月14日|Q2922}}の例祭宵宮では、現在も隼人舞が奉納される（京田辺市指定{{jalink|無形民俗文化財}}である{{Cite web|和書|title=「月読神社と隼人舞」|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=京田辺市|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=京田辺市}}）。 また、当神社が所在する「大住」という地名も九州の「{{jalink|大隅国|Q981002|大隅}}」に由来と考えられる{{Cite web|和書|title=大住隼人舞|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=京田辺市教育委員会}}。 === 境内 === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|境内 === 交通アクセス === *{{jalink|学研都市線}}（{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}）{{jalink|大住駅|Q589844}}から徒歩15分 ===脚注=== {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ 大住隼人舞]-京田辺市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]

= [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京田辺市|Q816728}}大住池平31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ツクヨミ|Q595520|月読命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（大） |創建 = {{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月15日|Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=月讀神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''月讀神社'''（つきよみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京田辺市|Q816728}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}。'''{{jalink|隼人舞|Q55529681}}'''の発祥地として知られる。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}）社殿を平城京より平安京に遷した際、大住山において霊光を拝し、この地に社殿を建立したのを創建とする。{{jalink|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|Q1193959}}の際は、戦火を避けるため{{jalink|石清水八幡宮|Q710098}}が一時遷座され、御神宝が薬師堂に安置された。 === 神階 === *{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から正五位下（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「樺井月読神」 *{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}6年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）、従一位（社伝） === 隼人舞 === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|隼人舞発祥之碑}}]] {{jalink|九州|Q13987}}南部の大隅{{jalink|隼人|Q1151764}}が{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}大住に移住し、この大住隼人が郷土の民俗芸能の隼人舞をこの月讀神社に奉納して舞伝えてきたという伝承から、隼人舞の発祥地とされている。{{jalink|10月14日|Q2922}}の例祭宵宮では、現在も隼人舞が奉納される（京田辺市指定{{jalink|無形民俗文化財}}である{{Cite web|和書|title=「月読神社と隼人舞」|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=京田辺市|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=京田辺市}}）。 また、当神社が所在する「大住」という地名も九州の「{{jalink|大隅国|Q981002|大隅}}」に由来と考えられる{{Cite web|和書|title=大住隼人舞|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=京田辺市教育委員会}}。 === 境内 === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|境内 === 交通アクセス === *{{jalink|学研都市線}}（{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}）{{jalink|大住駅|Q589844}}から徒歩15分 ===脚注=== {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ 大住隼人舞]-京田辺市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}

(Japan) (P3225) == * 7130005009083 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市大住池平31', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:月讀神社 (京田辺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 月讀神社 |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京田辺市|Q816728}}大住池平31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ツクヨミ|Q595520|月読命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（大） |創建 = {{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月15日|Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=月讀神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''月讀神社'''（つきよみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京田辺市|Q816728}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}。'''{{jalink|隼人舞|Q55529681}}'''の発祥地として知られる。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}）社殿を平城京より平安京に遷した際、大住山において霊光を拝し、この地に社殿を建立したのを創建とする。{{jalink|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|Q1193959}}の際は、戦火を避けるため{{jalink|石清水八幡宮|Q710098}}が一時遷座され、御神宝が薬師堂に安置された。 === 神階 === *{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から正五位下（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「樺井月読神」 *{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}6年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）、従一位（社伝） === 隼人舞 === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|隼人舞発祥之碑}}]] {{jalink|九州|Q13987}}南部の大隅{{jalink|隼人|Q1151764}}が{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}大住に移住し、この大住隼人が郷土の民俗芸能の隼人舞をこの月讀神社に奉納して舞伝えてきたという伝承から、隼人舞の発祥地とされている。{{jalink|10月14日|Q2922}}の例祭宵宮では、現在も隼人舞が奉納される（京田辺市指定{{jalink|無形民俗文化財}}である{{Cite web|和書|title=「月読神社と隼人舞」|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=京田辺市|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=京田辺市}}）。 また、当神社が所在する「大住」という地名も九州の「{{jalink|大隅国|Q981002|大隅}}」に由来と考えられる{{Cite web|和書|title=大住隼人舞|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=京田辺市教育委員会}}。 === 境内 === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|境内 === 交通アクセス === *{{jalink|学研都市線}}（{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}）{{jalink|大住駅|Q589844}}から徒歩15分 ===脚注=== {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ 大住隼人舞]-京田辺市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}

[[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''月讀神社'''（つきよみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京田辺市|Q816728}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}。'''{{jalink|隼人舞|Q55529681}}'''の発祥地として知られる。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}4年（{{jalink|809年|Q30671}}）社殿を平城京より平安京に遷した際、大住山において霊光を拝し、この地に社殿を建立したのを創建とする。{{jalink|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|Q1193959}}の際は、戦火を避けるため{{jalink|石清水八幡宮|Q710098}}が一時遷座され、御神宝が薬師堂に安置された。 === 神階 === *{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から正五位下（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「樺井月読神」 *{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}6年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）、従一位（社伝） === 隼人舞 === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|隼人舞発祥之碑}}]] {{jalink|九州|Q13987}}南部の大隅{{jalink|隼人|Q1151764}}が{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}大住に移住し、この大住隼人が郷土の民俗芸能の隼人舞をこの月讀神社に奉納して舞伝えてきたという伝承から、隼人舞の発祥地とされている。{{jalink|10月14日|Q2922}}の例祭宵宮では、現在も隼人舞が奉納される（京田辺市指定{{jalink|無形民俗文化財}}である{{Cite web|和書|title=「月読神社と隼人舞」|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=京田辺市|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=京田辺市}}）。 また、当神社が所在する「大住」という地名も九州の「{{jalink|大隅国|Q981002|大隅}}」に由来と考えられる{{Cite web|和書|title=大住隼人舞|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=京田辺市教育委員会}}。 === 境内 === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|境内 === 交通アクセス === *{{jalink|学研都市線}}（{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}）{{jalink|大住駅|Q589844}}から徒歩15分 ===脚注=== {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ 大住隼人舞]-京田辺市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}

(Japan) (P3225) == * 7130005009083 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市大住池平31', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:月讀神社 (京田辺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyōtanabe|Q816728}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Tsukuyomi|Q595520|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（great） |創建 = {{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 4 ({{jalink|809|Q30671}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 15|Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kyōtanabe|Q816728}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{jalink|Hayato-mai|Q55529681}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{jalink|809|Q30671}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{jalink|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959}}, {{jalink|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 6 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} from southern {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{jalink|October 14|Q2922}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{jalink|Ōsumi Province|Q981002|Ōsumi}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Ōsumi Station|Q589844}} on the {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} ({{jalink|JR West|Q502125}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}

Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. At the eve of the annual festival on {{jalink|October 14|Q2922}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{jalink|Ōsumi Province|Q981002|Ōsumi}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Ōsumi Station|Q589844}} on the {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} ({{jalink|JR West|Q502125}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyōtanabe|Q816728}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Tsukuyomi|Q595520|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（great） |創建 = {{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 4 ({{jalink|809|Q30671}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 15|Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kyōtanabe|Q816728}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{jalink|Hayato-mai|Q55529681}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{jalink|809|Q30671}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{jalink|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959}}, {{jalink|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 6 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} from southern {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{jalink|October 14|Q2922}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{jalink|Ōsumi Province|Q981002|Ōsumi}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Ōsumi Station|Q589844}} on the {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} ({{jalink|JR West|Q502125}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}

During the {{jalink|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959}}, {{jalink|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 6 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} from southern {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{jalink|October 14|Q2922}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{jalink|Ōsumi Province|Q981002|Ōsumi}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Ōsumi Station|Q589844}} on the {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} ({{jalink|JR West|Q502125}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}

Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyōtanabe|Q816728}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Tsukuyomi|Q595520|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（great） |創建 = {{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 4 ({{jalink|809|Q30671}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 15|Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kyōtanabe|Q816728}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{jalink|Hayato-mai|Q55529681}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{jalink|809|Q30671}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{jalink|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959}}, {{jalink|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 6 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} from southern {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{jalink|October 14|Q2922}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{jalink|Ōsumi Province|Q981002|Ōsumi}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Ōsumi Station|Q589844}} on the {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} ({{jalink|JR West|Q502125}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}

It is located in [[Yamashiro Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická (Japan) (P3225) == * 7130005009083 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市大住池平31', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:月讀神社 (京田辺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyōtanabe|Q816728}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Tsukuyomi|Q595520|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（great） |創建 = {{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 4 ({{jalink|809|Q30671}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 15|Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kyōtanabe|Q816728}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{jalink|Hayato-mai|Q55529681}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{jalink|809|Q30671}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{jalink|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959}}, {{jalink|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 6 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} from southern {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{jalink|October 14|Q2922}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{jalink|Ōsumi Province|Q981002|Ōsumi}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Ōsumi Station|Q589844}} on the {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} ({{jalink|JR West|Q502125}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}（great） |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{ill|lt=Hayato-mai|Q55529681|WD=Q55529681}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{ill|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959|WD=Q1193959}}, {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 6 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764}} from southern {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{ill|lt=Ōsumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ōsumi Station|Q589844|WD=Q589844}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（great） |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{ill|lt=Hayato-mai|Q55529681|WD=Q55529681}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{ill|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959|WD=Q1193959}}, {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 6 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764}} from southern {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{ill|lt=Ōsumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ōsumi Station|Q589844|WD=Q589844}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（great） |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{ill|lt=Hayato-mai|Q55529681|WD=Q55529681}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{ill|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959|WD=Q1193959}}, {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 6 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764}} from southern {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{ill|lt=Ōsumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ōsumi Station|Q589844|WD=Q589844}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|WD=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: kana (P1814) == * つきよみじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7130005009083 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市大住池平31', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:月讀神社 (京田辺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great） |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671|1=809}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{ill|lt=Hayato-mai|Q55529681|WD=Q55529681|1=Hayato-mai}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671|1=809}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{ill|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959|WD=Q1193959|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}}, {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} from southern {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987|1=Kyushu}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{ill|lt=Ōsumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi|1=Ōsumi Province}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ōsumi Station|Q589844|WD=Q589844|1=Ōsumi Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

kana (P1814) == * つきよみじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7130005009083 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市大住池平31', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:月讀神社 (京田辺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q54153263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great） |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671|1=809}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{ill|lt=Hayato-mai|Q55529681|WD=Q55529681|1=Hayato-mai}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671|1=809}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{ill|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959|WD=Q1193959|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}}, {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} from southern {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987|1=Kyushu}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{ill|lt=Ōsumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi|1=Ōsumi Province}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ōsumi Station|Q589844|WD=Q589844|1=Ōsumi Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)}}[[Category:qq]]

link|Q54153263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great） |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671|1=809}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{ill|lt=Hayato-mai|Q55529681|WD=Q55529681|1=Hayato-mai}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671|1=809}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{ill|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959|WD=Q1193959|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}}, {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} from southern {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987|1=Kyushu}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{ill|lt=Ōsumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi|1=Ōsumi Province}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ōsumi Station|Q589844|WD=Q589844|1=Ōsumi Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

link|Q54153263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great） |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671|1=809}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{ill|lt=Hayato-mai|Q55529681|WD=Q55529681|1=Hayato-mai}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671|1=809}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{ill|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959|WD=Q1193959|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}}, {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} from southern {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987|1=Kyushu}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{ill|lt=Ōsumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi|1=Ōsumi Province}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ōsumi Station|Q589844|WD=Q589844|1=Ōsumi Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Kyotanabe, Kyoto]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

During the {{ill|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959|WD=Q1193959|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}}, {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} from southern {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987|1=Kyushu}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{ill|lt=Ōsumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi|1=Ōsumi Province}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ōsumi Station|Q589844|WD=Q589844|1=Ōsumi Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Kyotanabe, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Ōsumi Ikehira 31 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great） |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671|1=809}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and is known as the birthplace of '''{{ill|lt=Hayato-mai|Q55529681|WD=Q55529681|1=Hayato-mai}}'''. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, when the shrine buildings were moved from Heijō-kyō to Heian-kyō in {{ill|lt=809|Q30671|WD=Q30671|1=809}} (Daidō 4), divine light was seen on Mt. Ōsumi, and building a shrine at this place marks the founding. During the {{ill|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Q1193959|WD=Q1193959|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}}, {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} was temporarily relocated here to avoid warfare, and sacred treasures were placed in Yakushi Hall. === Court rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: from Junior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kabai Tsukuyomi-no-Kami" *{{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}): Junior First Rank (shrine tradition) === Hayato-mai === [[ファイル:Hyatomai Denshouti.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Monument of the birthplace of Hayato-mai}}]] From a tradition that Ōsumi {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} from southern {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987|1=Kyushu}} migrated to Ōsumi in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, and that these Ōsumi Hayato dedicated and transmitted Hayato-mai here, this shrine is regarded as the birthplace of Hayato-mai. At the eve of the annual festival on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, Hayato-mai is still dedicated (designated an intangible folk cultural property of Kyōtanabe City{{Cite web|和書|title="Tsukiyomi Shrine and Hayato-mai"|url=http://www.kyotanabe.jp/html/kanko/i/tsukiyomi.htm|website=Kyōtanabe City|accessdate=2019-09-20|language=ja|last=Kyōtanabe City}}). Also, the place name "Ōsumi" where this shrine is located is thought to derive from "{{ill|lt=Ōsumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi|1=Ōsumi Province}}" in Kyushu{{Cite web|和書|title=Ōsumi Hayato-mai|url=http://www.kyotanabe.ed.jp/nc24/culture/htdocs/mu6y5636h-117/|website=www.kyotanabe.ed.jp|accessdate=2020-07-03|publisher=Kyōtanabe City Board of Education}}. === Precincts === ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tsukiyomi Jinja Kyotanabe Keidai.jpg|Precincts === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ōsumi Station|Q589844|WD=Q589844|1=Ōsumi Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}}) ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://kyotana.be/object/detail/119/ Ōsumi Hayato-mai] - Kyōtanabe Tourism Association {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Q54153263)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Kyotanabe, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]]
